mission.title: Install and Configure Backstage on Kubernetes
mission.description: Backstage is an open framework for building developer portals that helps organizations manage their software catalog and streamline development processes. Installing Backstage allows teams to unify their infrastructure tooling and enhance productivity.

steps.title: Install Backstage via Helm
steps.description:
Add the Backstage Helm chart repository and install:
```bash
helm repo add backstage https://backstage.github.io/charts
helm repo update
kubectl create namespace backstage
helm install backstage backstage/backstage --namespace backstage --version 2.4.0
```

steps.title: Configure the app-config
steps.description:
Create a ConfigMap with your Backstage configuration:
```bash
kubectl apply -n backstage -f - << 'EOF'
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: backstage-app-config
data:
  app-config.yaml: |
    app:
      title: My Backstage
      baseUrl: http://localhost:7007
    backend:
      baseUrl: http://localhost:7007
      database:
        client: better-sqlite3
        connection: ':memory:'
EOF
```

steps.title: Access the Backstage UI
steps.description:
Port-forward to access the Backstage dashboard:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/backstage -n backstage 7007:7007
```
Open http://localhost:7007

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n backstage
kubectl get svc -n backstage
```

uninstall.title: Remove Backstage
uninstall.description:
```bash
helm uninstall backstage -n backstage
kubectl delete namespace backstage
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade via Helm
upgrade.description:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade backstage backstage/backstage --namespace backstage
```

troubleshooting.title: Backstage pod not starting
troubleshooting.description:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=backstage -n backstage
```
Common issue: missing app-config.yaml configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Database connection errors
troubleshooting.description:
For production, use PostgreSQL instead of SQLite:
```bash
helm upgrade backstage backstage/backstage --set postgresql.enabled=true -n backstage
```

troubleshooting.title: Plugin loading errors
troubleshooting.description: Check the Backstage container logs for missing dependencies or configuration.

resolution.summary: Backstage developer portal is installed and accessible via the web UI.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and the necessary tools installed on your local machine.
